commentary_text: The model favors Japan to win at 69%, while the sportsbook consensus stands at 50%—a 19-percentage-point gap on the favorite outcome. This substantial divergence, paired with the market's broader hedging across all three outcomes, suggests bettors may be pricing in recent form, injuries, or lineup changes that occurred after the model's last update. The consensus also assigns Sweden nearly triple the win probability the model does, indicating real-money flows may reflect information about squad availability or recent performance unavailable to the statistical forecast. The dashboard surfaces this gap as a signal worth monitoring rather than a judgment on which assessment will prove correct.
